text.title: feedback-management Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for feedback-management as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLM categorization drifts: same feedback labelled "billing" on Monday and "pricing" on Friday unless the taxonomy is pinned in the prompt and validated.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin the taxonomy in both the system prompt and in a JSON schema; reject any agent output with tags outside the enum.

failure-mode.description: Duplicate detection on embeddings is fragile for short messages ("doesn't work", "broken") — needs context window from the source thread.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require a length floor (~30 chars) or include thread context in the dedup agent's input.

failure-mode.description: Aggregation by mention count rewards the loud minority; weight by segment ARR + retention risk or it just reproduces the squeaky-wheel bias.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force a segment-weighted secondary ranking. Top requests = [sum(mentions × weight_segment × weight_arr)].

failure-mode.description: Auto-generated "you asked, we built" emails sound robotic and erode trust if the user's verbatim is paraphrased away.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Keep the original quote in the email body. Close-loop drafts must reference verbatim. Keep drafts as *drafts* in the human's inbox, never auto-send.

failure-mode.description: Feedback from prospects (sales calls) and churned users cannot be replied to via the same close-the-loop flow; agents conflate them unless lifecycle stage is a required tag.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lifecycle stage must be a required input field (prospect, active, churned). Route each through different close-loop templates.
